spec_match.rationale: Uses `sr.audioTrack = track` (third API-shape variant across the feature), and runs two recognizers off a cloned single mic track — which is technically two recognizers on two tracks, but they're carrying the same audio so they only differ by language.

interactive.rationale: Real per-source language picker, per-source start/stop, live captions per panel, and a merged transcript labelled by source. Probe pills also surface the `audioTrack in SR.prototype` capability check.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises two recognizers each with a different language and its own track, merging into a labelled shared transcript — the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles `audioTrack not in prototype` (warn state) and mic-denied (per-panel `denied` tag), but doesn't show what happens if the cloned track ends or if both recognizers race on the same final result.

references.rationale: The page has no `references` or `see also` section linking to chromestatus.com/feature/<id>. The cleanup script needs this link to recover the folder name; without it the page would be deleted by /tmp/fix-slugs.py.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables and `color-mix(in srgb, ...)`; action buttons override both background and color; no static cards; mobile collapses sources-grid to single column.

openQuestions.title: Add a references / see-also section with the chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page MUST include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link — it's the self-heal mechanism the slug-recovery script relies on. Add a `references` section linking 5178378197139456 plus the WICG spec.

openQuestions.title: Use genuinely different audio sources, not a cloned mic
openQuestions.detail: Cloning the mic track gives both recognizers the same audio. The killer use case is two participants from a WebRTC call. Replace source B with a synthesised OscillatorNode→MediaStreamDestination audio track, or load a short audio file via `<audio>.captureStream()`, so each recognizer is reading genuinely different audio.

openQuestions.title: Reconcile API shape (this page uses `audioTrack`)
openQuestions.detail: Sibling concepts use `rec.start(track)`, `rec.mediaStreamTrack = track`, and this one uses `rec.audioTrack = track`. At most one is correct. Verify against the WICG spec PR and update siblings to match the canonical form.

openQuestions.title: Add the `SpeechRecognitionEvent.audioTrack` echo
openQuestions.detail: The info box claims `audioTrack on SpeechRecognitionEvent tells you which track fired the result`. The result handler doesn't actually use that property — it identifies source by which captionEl was bound at recognizer-creation time. Show the event-side property in action so authors can see how routing-by-event works.

summary: Best-structured demo of the feature, but it's missing the chromestatus link (a hard requirement), and it fakes the multi-source story by cloning a single mic. Also uses a third spelling of the API (`audioTrack`) — sibling concepts disagree.
